mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.91% is near historical lows, constraining further easing. Any signal of persistent inflation via CPIAUCSL acceleration above 2.5% YoY will trigger Fed tightening expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as market participants price in future rate increases and demand compensation for inflation risk.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL monthly increase > 0.3% or YoY inflation expectations exceed 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.91% near zero lower bound
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines despite inflation acceleration, or CPIAUCSL fails to accelerate above 2.5% YoY within 60 days of trigger activation. Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS at 146,135k) combined with unemployment rate at 4.3% signals a tightening labor market. Sustained monthly payroll gains above 200k for 3 consecutive months will compress wage growth expectations, increasing inflation risk premium and pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as the Fed faces pressure to normalize rates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly increase > 200k for 3 consecutive months while UNRATE remains below 4.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite sustained strong payroll growth and low unemployment. Payroll growth fails to exceed 200k for 3 consecutive months. Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 473,808M) represent current consumer demand strength. A sustained decline in RSAFS of more than 2% month-over-month signals weakening aggregate demand and recession risk, causing investors to flee to safety and demand lower yields, pushing 10-year Treasury yields down as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 473,808M indicates robust consumer spending
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises despite RSAFS decline > 2%. RSAFS fails to decline > 2% within 30 days of signal generation. Positive 20-day returns occur in > 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 105.03) reflects supply-side capacity utilization. A sustained decline in INDPRO below 104.5 over 2 consecutive months signals supply-side weakness and deflationary pressure, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as inflation expectations recede and real rates compress.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises despite sustained INDPRO decline. INDPRO remains above 104.5 for more than 1 month. Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1,172k) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure demand momentum. When both HOUST declines > 5% and RSAFS declines > 3% simultaneously, this signals broad-based demand destruction, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall sharply as growth expectations collapse and safe-haven demand increases.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% AND RSAFS declines > 3% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,172k and RSAFS at 473,808M show solid demand
regime_hint: demand shock or financial stress regime
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises despite simultaneous HOUST and RSAFS declines. Joint decline condition fails to trigger within 45 days. Negative 20-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.91% reflects accommodative policy. A rise in FEDFUNDS above 1.5% within 3 months signals Fed tightening cycle initiation. This tightening expectation will cause 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the market reprices the entire yield curve upward in anticipation of higher short-term rates and reduced monetary accommodation.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite FEDFUNDS rising above 1.5%. FEDFUNDS fails to exceed 1.5% within 90 days. Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.3% is near cyclical lows. A rise in UNRATE above 4.8% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as investors seek safe-haven assets and growth expectations compress, reducing long-term rate expectations.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises despite UNRATE exceeding 4.8%. UNRATE fails to exceed 4.8% within 20 trading days. Positive 5-day returns occur in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 16,861.6B) and payroll employment (PAYEMS at 146,135k) jointly measure economic growth momentum. When GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2% annualized AND PAYEMS growth slows below 100k monthly, this signals late-cycle deceleration, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as terminal rate expectations decline and growth premium compresses.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% AND PAYEMS monthly growth < 100k for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,861.6B and PAYEMS at 146,135k show solid growth
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration or growth slowdown regime
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises despite joint GDPC1 and PAYEMS deceleration. Joint trigger condition fails to activate within 90 days. Negative 60-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances.
